text: Original Abstract The self-attention mechanism, which was proposed for natural language processing, has gradually become a hot research direction in the computer vision field in recent years. However, for images, the application of self-attention is also restricted by three aspects: 1) taking images as 1D sequences will ignore the 2D information of images; 2) the high computational complexity, which is proportional to the square of the length of the sequence, will lead to severe performance bottleneck in the case of large images; 3) the self-attention mechanism can only show the spatial adaptability, while lacking the channel adaptability. In this paper, we propose a new linear attention called large kernel attention (LKA), and based on it, design a new network structure called Visual Attention Network (VAN). To our surprise, the VAN is very simple, but experimental results show that the models of the same scale even exceed the performance of many popular CNNs and ViTs in the tasks of image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, panoptic segmentation, and keypoint estimation, such as achieving 87.8% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with VAN-B6, and even achieving the state-of-the-art result of 58.2 PQ on panoptic segmentation, and exceeding Swin-T 4mIoU by 4 on ADE20K semantic segmentation, and exceeding the AP of Swin-T on COCO object detection by 2.6, etc. The code is available at https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network .
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written

In this paper, we propose a new linear attention called large kernel attention (LKA), and based on it, design a new network structure called Visual Attention Network (VAN).
